tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad Financial and Ridership Reports – October 2022. Steven Weiss, Financial Liaison.

payload.title: Metro-North Railroad Financial and Ridership Reports – October 2022
payload.description: Metro-North Railroad financial and ridership report for October 2022, including Mid-Year Forecast vs actuals, budget performance, staffing levels, and performance indicators.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Total Revenue YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Total revenue of $555.8 million was $47.4 million lower than the Mid-Year Forecast (Forecast).

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total revenue of $555.8 million

tool_args.label: Total Ridership YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Through October 2022 ridership was 39.6 million

evidence_refs.source_quote: ridership was 39.6 million

tool_args.label: Farebox Revenue YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Consequently, farebox revenue of $362.0 million was $27.7 million higher than the Forecast.

evidence_refs.source_quote: farebox revenue of $362.0 million

tool_args.label: Total Expenses before Non-Cash Liability Adjustments YTD October 2022

tool_args.label: Total Headcount YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: At the end of October, total headcount was 6,194, which was 333 lower than the Forecast of 6,528.

tool_args.label: Ridership Change vs 2021 YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Through October 2022 ridership was 39.6 million, which was 65.8% above 2021

tool_args.raw_text: 45.1% below 2019 pre-COVID levels (adjusted for the same number of workdays)

tool_args.label: Commutation Ridership YTD October 2022 and Change vs 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Commutation ridership of 14.9 million was 135.3% above 2021 and 8.1% above the Forecast.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Commutation ridership of 14.9 million was 135.3% above 2021

tool_args.label: Non-Commutation Ridership YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Non-Commutation ridership of 24.7 million was 41.1% above 2021 and 4.3% above the Forecast.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: The year-to-date October Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio was 35.2%, which is above the Forecast due to higher farebox revenue and lower expenses.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The year-to-date October Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio was 35.2%

tool_args.label: Adjusted Cost per Passenger YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: The year-to-date October Adjusted Cost per Passenger was $28.62, which is lower than the Forecast due to higher ridership and lower expenses.

tool_args.label: Non-Reimbursable Overtime Unfavorable Variance YTD October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Overtime expenses were $17.6 million unfavorable to the Forecast primarily due to coverage required for must fill open positions.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Overtime expenses were $17.6 million unfavorable to the Forecast

tool_args.label: Total Revenue Lower than Forecast - Capital Reimbursements
tool_args.raw_text: Total revenue of $555.8 million was $47.4 million lower than the Mid-Year Forecast (Forecast). This results from lower Capital Reimbursements partially offset by higher farebox revenue.

payload.claim_text: Total revenue of $555.8 million was $47.4 million lower than the Mid-Year Forecast due to lower Capital Reimbursements partially offset by higher farebox revenue.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad Total Revenue

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total revenue of $555.8 million was $47.4 million lower than the Mid-Year Forecast (Forecast). This results from lower Capital Reimbursements partially offset by higher farebox revenue.

tool_args.label: Total Expenses Favorable to Forecast
tool_args.raw_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $1,357.6 million were $88.5 million or 6.1% favorable to the Forecast.

payload.claim_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $1,357.6 million were $88.5 million or 6.1% favorable to the Forecast. Primary drivers: timing of expenses for material usage, lower professional service contracts and energy costs, and vacant positions partially offset by higher overtime and pension costs.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad Total Expenses

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $1,357.6 million were $88.5 million or 6.1% favorable to the Forecast. The primary drivers of this favorable variance are the timing of expenses for material usage, lower professional service contracts and energy costs, and vacant positions partially offset by higher overtime and pension costs.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Non-Reimbursable Operating Results Favorable to Forecast
tool_args.raw_text: October YTD Non-Reimbursable operating results were favorable to forecast by $57.2 million or 5.2%.

payload.claim_text: October YTD Non-Reimbursable operating results were favorable to forecast by $57.2 million or 5.2%. Non-Reimbursable revenues through October were $26.2 million favorable due to higher Farebox Revenue. Total Non-Reimbursable expenses were $14.8 favorable.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad Non-Reimbursable Operations

evidence_refs.source_quote: October YTD Non-Reimbursable operating results were favorable to forecast by $57.2 million or 5.2%.

tool_args.label: Total Overtime Unfavorable to Forecast
tool_args.raw_text: Total overtime was $7.6 million unfavorable to the Forecast. Non-Reimbursable was $17.6 million unfavorable and Reimbursable was $10.0 million favorable.

payload.claim_text: Total overtime was $7.6 million unfavorable to the Forecast. Non-Reimbursable was $17.6 million unfavorable and Reimbursable was $10.0 million favorable. Unfavorable Non-Reimbursable overtime was primarily driven by the Maintenance of Equipment Department, which was unfavorable due to vacancy/absentee coverage.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad Overtime

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total overtime was $7.6 million unfavorable to the Forecast.

tool_args.label: Largest Number of Vacancies by Department
tool_args.raw_text: The largest number of vacancies were in Maintenance of Equipment (111), Maintenance of Way (80), various Administrative (50), and Customer Service (46).

payload.claim_text: The largest number of vacancies were in Maintenance of Equipment (111), Maintenance of Way (80), various Administrative (50), and Customer Service (46). The vacancies are primarily split between the Operational Hourlies and the Professional/Technical/Clerical categories.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad Vacancies

evidence_refs.source_quote: The largest number of vacancies were in Maintenance of Equipment (111), Maintenance of Way (80), various Administrative (50), and Customer Service (46).
